verdict_reasoning: ITPR3 (inositol 1,4,5-trisphosphate receptor type 3) is a ligand-gated calcium channel resident on the endoplasmic reticulum membrane, with both its N-terminal IP3-binding domain and C-terminal channel domain facing the cytoplasm. It is a multi-pass TM protein anchored in the ER membrane, not the plasma membrane. Checking contextual buckets: (1) cell_state_induced — no documented stress/activation-induced surface translocation; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — no evidence of PM display in any lineage; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — ITPR3 is ER-resident, not lysosomal; (4) dual_localization — no credible evidence of a stable PM pool from surface biotinylation or flow cytometry on intact cells; (5) stable_surface_attachment — secreted isoform with wash-resistant PM anchoring not reported. No antibody/ADC/CAR-T therapeutic program targets ITPR3 on the cell surface. The protein is unambiguously endomembrane-resident with cytoplasmic-facing functional domains and no mechanism placing it on the outer leaflet of the PM.
